agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 with SSTR2 ECL2 (res 192/193/19

report.hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 with SSTR2 ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by strengthening subtype-specific contacts while reducing off-target affinity.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by disrupting c

report.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by disrupting conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the core pharmacophore. Iteration 20 supports this hypothesis, but further refinement is needed to push Δmargin above 9 for more candidates.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions with off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 while avoiding disruption of the pharmacophore. Acidic and polar residues are introduced to break hydrophobic and hydrogen-bonding interactions in off-target pockets.
